provider.name: Amy Thompson
provider.address: 2015 W Broadway St, Unit 3A, Ardmore, OK 73401

context: Month 18 of 24 post-Hansen bilateral rib reconstruction (Day ~525)
DISCHARGE / TRANSITION SESSION — final formal PT visit with Amy Thompson
Transitioning to independent gym-based rehab + Amy Thompson (Excel Therapy) ASTYM/IASTM consultation
Ryan available PRN via Malcolm at Physical Therapy Central (580) 247-1320
Session compressed by scheduling conflict; RDL 'Jack protocol' and exercise triage not completed

concerns.title: Left scapular winging under bilateral load
concerns.detail: Present and acknowledged during suspension/cable row — 'It's winging. It cheats because it's weaker.' Weight reduced to preserve form. Consistent with established SICK scapula pattern. See vault notes.

concerns.title: Hip strength vs balance strategy
concerns.detail: New coaching theme: hip sway as compensatory balance strategy rather than intrinsic hip stabilization. Sets up next phase of independent work. See vault notes.

concerns.title: FiberTape suture / cheese-wire concern
concerns.detail: Ryan raised theoretical concern about FiberTape cutting through cartilage over time. Not an acute finding; flagged for next Dr. Hansen consult.

sinceLastSession: Session 10 on Mar 17 — rectus abdominis symmetry breakthrough reported; IASTM philosophy discussion consumed ~15 minutes
Resistance bands still not purchased (unresolved across all 11 sessions)
findings: Rib visual assessment positive: 'Your ribs look good. Like them. They're not flaring. They're not extending.' — most explicit rib status confirmation in 11 sessions (visual, not formal exam).
Session 1 rotational/squeeze exercise revisited and clearly improved — objective evidence of trajectory over 11 sessions.
Left scapular winging present under bilateral load; load reduction preserved form.
Hip sway compensatory strategy identified as next-phase focus.
Thread the needle deferred for 6th consecutive session; may never be formally demonstrated given discharge.
prescribed: No new exercises — existing Session 9-10 protocol continues
Limber Health program (code ZAS-EWV-IFQ) — 5 exercises
Mirror-based form monitoring at gym
Balloon/diaphragmatic breathing daily
notes: DISCHARGE session — independent gym protocol handoff. Amy Thompson (Excel Therapy, (580) 226-9235) consult queued for ASTYM/IASTM. Ryan available PRN via Malcolm. No formal discharge documentation; informal self-directed transition. See canonical vault note for full clinical detail: atlas/health/pt-session-2026-03-20-notes-with-ryan.md.

provider.name: Amy Thompson
provider.address: 2015 W Broadway St, Unit 3A, Ardmore, OK 73401

context: Month 17 of 24 post-Hansen bilateral rib reconstruction (Day ~522)
Ryan's clinical reframing: ribs are no longer the limiting factor — rehab challenge has shifted to neuromotor retraining of compensations
Patient-reported rectus abdominis symmetry breakthrough — most significant core recovery milestone since surgery
IASTM philosophy discussion consumed ~15 minutes of session time

concerns.title: Persistent thoracic extension compensation
concerns.detail: Dominant movement fault across all exercises — required three distinct corrections during session (heel push, lunge, single-leg RDL). See vault notes.

concerns.title: Plate debris sensory experience during hip flexion
concerns.detail: Patient characterization: 'as soon as I flex my hip joint, all the trash in the bottom of the garbage can is floating up.' Expected but distressing during month 17 of PLLA/PLGA degradation.

concerns.title: IASTM philosophy divergence
concerns.detail: Ryan does not believe tool-based scar tissue separation works mechanically; views as neurophysiological only. Resolution: self-apply at home, not billable. See vault notes.

sinceLastSession: Cervical stiffness from sleeping outdoors during OK temperature swing (40s to near 90F in 48h)
Rectus abdominis symmetry breakthrough reported — first symmetric core engagement since surgery
Improved proprioceptive literacy: patient independently detected core cheating during arm bike rotation
findings: Rectus abdominis symmetry breakthrough — 'top to bottom... I could feel the two center columns.' Suggests neural recovery progressing despite ongoing plate degradation.
Ryan's reframing: 'I think the ribs are in a good spot... your body doesn't know how to move without the compensations now.' Rehab shifted from structural protection to neuromotor retraining.
Thoracic extension compensation persists as dominant movement fault but proprioceptive awareness improving.
Isometric (yoga) vs full-ROM tendon conditioning distinction clarified — tendon strength requires eccentric loading through full range.
prescribed: Wall rotational hip exercise with ball — 8 reps bilateral daily
Side-lying hip external rotation — 8 reps each side, 3-5x/week
Lunge with anterior weight distribution (glute focus) — 3-5x/week
Single-leg RDL with thoracic rotation — 3-5 reps/leg, 3-5x/week
Supine retention with ball — 8-10 reps, 3-5x/week
Continue Limber Health program (code ZAS-EWV-IFQ)
notes: Ryan's clearest statement yet that rehab has shifted from rib-protection to neuromotor retraining. Rectus abdominis symmetry breakthrough is clinically significant — first symmetric core engagement in 17 months. Thread the needle deferred for 5th time. Resistance bands still not acquired. See canonical vault note for full clinical detail: atlas/health/pt-session-2026-03-17-notes-with-ryan.md.

provider.name: Amy Thompson
provider.address: 2015 W Broadway St, Unit 3A, Ardmore, OK 73401

context: Month 17 of 24 post-Hansen bilateral rib reconstruction (Day ~521)
Critical collagen remodeling window (months 12-18) — we're IN IT
Extended recovery timeline: 29-41 months total (Hansen Sep 2025 phone consult)
Bioabsorbable plates at gravel-like stage — sutures intact, biological fusion forming
First objective ROM measurement in 9 sessions — 67% external rotation deficit
Strategic/academic session — Ryan believed farewell visit, oriented toward self-reliance

concerns.title: External rotation deficit (67%)
concerns.detail: ~60 degrees available vs 180 normal. Ryan attributes primarily to habitual internal rotation from piano playing, potentially predating rib surgery. First objective measurement in 9 sessions ($1,955 cumulative).

concerns.title: Internal rotation dominance — system-level avoidance
concerns.detail: Serratus anterior weakness reframed: not isolated strength deficit but whole-system avoidance pattern. Body learned to 'cheat' around serratus for 8+ years. First response to external rotation is thoracic extension ('lift up').

concerns.title: Upper rib compensation (ribs 2-5) — NEW FINDING
concerns.detail: Compensatory tension migrated upward from surgical site (ribs 9-10). Pectoralis minor driving rib compression as 'prepare for the flood' bracing response to lower rib instability.

concerns.title: Left-chain compensation cascade — expanded
concerns.detail: Full cascade articulated: labral tear (2018) → hip repair (2020) → bilateral rib instability → trunk/scapular collapse → thumb MCP. Jack's hypothesis: rib stability may make labral tear asymptomatic without surgery.

sinceLastSession: Gained access to overhead bar at father-in-law's garage gym — first pull-up/hang infrastructure since moving to Ardmore
Completed multi-model clinical analysis of all 8 sessions (Opus 4.6, o3, GPT-5.4, GPT-5.4 Pro) — validated left-chain model, graded Ryan A-
Understanding of tensegrity model deepened through research and synthesis of operative report
Showed Ryan the AI-generated clinical analysis and operative report diagrams — Ryan: 'It's pretty legit, right?'
Resistance bands STILL not acquired — 5th session deferral at that point
findings: First objective ROM measurement: external rotation ~60 degrees vs 180 normal (67% deficit). Loss begins around 110 degrees with significant restriction.
Piano playing identified as primary driver of internal rotation compensation and serratus weakness — potentially predating rib injury entirely
Serratus weakness is system-level avoidance, not isolated strength deficit — 'much bigger than just strengthening the serratus'
Upper rib (2-5) compensation pattern NEW: pec minor driving compression as bracing response despite surgery on lower ribs (9-10)
Tensegrity model synthesized with Ryan — breathing IS the long-term load Hansen's model depends on. Ryan's loading philosophy aligns with Hansen's approach.
Nerve regeneration confirmed at 16 months — ablated nerves regrown, remaining symptoms motor not sensory
Ryan was preparing Jack for independence (farewell confusion) — didn't charge for session. Confusion resolved mid-session; treatment resumes Tuesday.
Bioabsorbable plates at gravel-like stage — sutures intact per Hansen Sep 2025 assessment. Jack hypothesizes ADHD medication oxidative stress may have accelerated degradation.
prescribed: Pull-ups and dead hangs — 10s holds, 10 reps, scapular engagement, no rib flare
Single-arm RDL — heavy weight left arm, 8-10 reps x 2-3 sets, hip hinge only
Landmine rotations — if equipment available, core bracing throughout
Resisted external rotation work — any 'resisted opening' movements
Limber Health app protocol (access code ZAS-EWV-IFQ) — rotational stability

morning: 15g collagen + 500mg Vitamin C + 20oz water before moving
Diaphragmatic reset: 10 min posterior rib breathing (crocodile position, 4-4-4 pattern)
Log roll exit from bed (no trunk rotation)
Peroneal release (R foot) — tennis ball under lateral foot, 2-3 min
Hip extension stretch — standing, 30sec x3 (releases psoas, stops anterior rib flare)
Pectoral engagement check (daily 0-10 metric)
protocol.supplements: Collagen 15g AM, Vitamin C 1000mg/day (500 x2), Magnesium 400mg PM, 80oz+ water
protocol.restrictions: No spinal rotation >45 degrees, no overhead reaching, no heavy lifting

exercises.name: Pull-ups and dead hangs
exercises.detail: 10-second holds, 10 reps. Scapular engagement, avoid rib flare. Progress +2-3 seconds weekly. Stop if clicking increases or sharp pain.

exercises.name: Single-arm Romanian deadlifts (RDL)
exercises.detail: Heavy weight left arm. Hip hinge 'windmill without rotation.' Handheld support OK initially. Ryan: 'Number two exercise for the glutes.'

exercises.name: Landmine rotations
exercises.detail: Fascial line activation with controlled rotation. Core bracing throughout. Contingent on equipment at father-in-law's gym.

exercises.name: Resisted external rotation work
exercises.detail: Any movements 'resisted opening.' Breaking habitual internal rotation from piano playing. Multiple angles, end-range emphasis.

exercises.name: Limber Health app — Side plank rotations
exercises.detail: Resisted Modified Side Plank Rotation — bilateral. Access code ZAS-EWV-IFQ.

exercises.name: Limber Health app — Standing row rotations
exercises.detail: Resisted Standing Row Rotation — bilateral.

exercises.name: Limber Health app — Brace extended reverse march
exercises.detail: Supported Brace Extended Reverse March.

exercises.name: Limber Health app — Foam roller thoracic extension
exercises.detail: Foam Roller Thoracic Extension.

exercises.name: Limber Health app — Kettlebell Turkish windmill
exercises.detail: Kettlebell Turkish Windmill — bilateral.

exercises.name: Rotational hip exercise with wall contact
exercises.detail: Push hip toward front door while rotating, coordinate with head positioning, heel against back wall

exercises.name: Core crunches with scapular initiation
exercises.detail: Start crunch between shoulder blades, pull hands toward knees — initiate from upper back not abdomen

exercises.name: Outside pinky wall push
exercises.detail: Push outside pinky finger up wall surface maintaining shoulder positioning — activates posterior chain

exercises.name: Mirror-based breathing observation
exercises.detail: Diaphragmatic breathing shirtless — visualize rib cage movement, expand ribs while keeping them down, prevent thoracic extension

exercises.name: Dead hangs with scapular focus
exercises.detail: Maintain shoulder blade engagement, avoid rib cage flaring, progress from supported to full hang

exercises.name: Wall-based rotational band work
exercises.detail: Orange band, inside arm stabilized against wall, outside arm rotating against resistance

exercises.name: Cross-body diagonal band pulls
exercises.detail: Discus/spiral pattern, mid-to-high level (NOT low), spiral fascial line activation

exercises.name: Backward pulling movements
exercises.detail: Posterior chain activation, maintain form

exercises.name: Balloon breathing
exercises.detail: Inflate using belly expansion NOT chest rise, eliminate lip pursing, complete exhale with abdominal contraction — 'quick exit' sound

exercises.name: Rotational marching
exercises.detail: Alternating leg marches maintaining abdominal tension, coordinate with breathing, prevent thoracic extension

exercises.name: Facing band same side march
exercises.detail: Band high and behind, march with straight arm extension — maintain fascial tension throughout ROM

exercises.name: Thread the needle
exercises.detail: To be demonstrated by Ryan

exercises.name: Spiral/rotational fascial work
exercises.detail: 'Reverse lawnmower' movement, mid-to-high level NOT low

exercises.name: Anterior chain fly + posterior control
exercises.detail: Dual-component, front and back simultaneously

exercises.name: Pull-apart exercises
exercises.detail: Maintain form and control

exercises.name: Anti-machine lengthening + posterior rotation
exercises.detail: Session 3 prescription

exercises.name: Serratus activation
exercises.detail: Prone position, wrists ahead of elbows

exercises.name: Core/rotation work
exercises.detail: Squeeze ball, contract core, rotate toward foot — ab compression NOT shoulder extension

exercises.name: Scap strengthening
exercises.detail: Dead hang position, maintain blade engagement — progress to alternating marches

exercises.name: Scap-ups
exercises.detail: Continue, avoid compensation patterns

exercises.name: Anti-rotation band work
exercises.detail: Modified walk-outs with super/thera band — isometric eccentric control, NOT active twisting

exercises.name: Transverse abdominis activation
exercises.detail: Supine, knees bent, draw belly button to spine, hold

questions: Confirm: 'I'm continuing with you AND exploring Amy as a complement' — prevent future scheduling confusion
Resistance bands — 6th deferral, non-negotiable for Session 10
Thread the needle demonstration — 5th deferral, must happen Session 10
Share shoulder MRI with Ryan — offered Session 9, not yet provided
Did Ryan call Dr. Hansen? (Still unanswered from Session 8)
Formal reassessment: differential click exam, QuickDASH/NPRS baseline, pectoral engagement re-measurement
Exercise triage: Ryan's 'more is better' vs multi-model 'trim to 5-7' — what's clinically optimal?
CPAP + unstable thoracic rings during sleep — 9 sessions, never addressed
Is PT curative for the labral tear or preparatory for eventual surgical repair?

careTeam.name: Amy Thompson, PT
careTeam.note: TO CONTACT — ask about integrated movement approach

decompressionReset: Do NOT push rib back. Lie on R side → reach L arm overhead → deep breath into L flank → let gravity + breath reset → hold 2-3 min.
notes: Session 9 was uniquely strategic/academic — Ryan believed farewell visit and oriented toward self-reliance. Didn't charge. Format produced clinical reasoning depth unavailable in hands-on sessions: tensegrity synthesis, loading philosophy alignment, upper rib compensation discovery. First ROM measurement in 9 sessions: 67% external rotation deficit, attributed primarily to piano playing. Farewell confusion resolved — Jack is continuing care. Multi-model analysis (Opus 4.6 + Sonnet 4.6) converged on central question: is PT curative or preparatory for the labral tear?

context: Month 17 of 24 post-Hansen bilateral rib reconstruction (Day ~515)
Critical collagen remodeling window (months 12-18) — we're IN IT

concerns.title: Breathing dysfunction
concerns.detail: Persistent thoracic extension compensation during breathing — diaphragm pulls down but unsecured ribs trigger elevation through low back extension. Foundational issue affecting all exercises.

concerns.title: Scapulothoracic interface dysfunction
concerns.detail: Left scapula elevated, 'bunch of fluff' tissue posterior preventing proper seating. Position-dependent clicking eliminated by forward lean.

concerns.title: Pectoral engagement severely limited (~2-3/10)
concerns.detail: Key recovery indicator; muscles guarding/spasming to protect unstable ribs. Compensatory patterns from 17 months post-surgery.

concerns.title: Left-chain compensation cascade
concerns.detail: Labral tear → rib instability → manual self-reorg → thumb MCP overload. Elite compensations from yoga teacher training deeply baked in.

sinceLastSession: Reviewed Dr. Hansen's surgical notes with Claude — extracted spacer graft details, confirmed rib 7 NOT involved
Discovered extended recovery timeline (29-41 months vs previously thought ~24)
Continued mirror-based diaphragmatic breathing work (shirt off)
Ongoing self-repositioning behavior — pushing ribs forward, bracing at coracoid/clavicle area
findings: Left scapular clicking during arm bike is strongly position-dependent — eliminated entirely by forward lean. Most reproducible symptom modifier in 8 sessions.
Left scapula remains elevated compared to right — persistent SICK scapula presentation
'Bunch of fluff' tissue posterior to scapula preventing scapulothoracic contact — fibrotic adhesion or altered muscle tone
Rib cage hypothesis: ribs backing into scapular space — pushing ribs forward manually gives shoulder blade space
AC joint involvement hypothesized (pec minor → coracoid → AC shear from self-repositioning) — NOT tested, no provocation tests performed
Ryan genuinely confused by Hansen's 3-year recovery timeline — volunteered to call Dr. Hansen
Breath-holding during exercises caught — mental engagement displacing respiratory awareness
Rotational hip exercise produced immediate postural improvement ('half inch taller' sensation)

provider.name: Amy Thompson
provider.address: 2015 W Broadway St, Unit 3A, Ardmore, OK 73401

context: Month 17 of 24 post-Hansen bilateral rib reconstruction (Day 508)
Breathing dysfunction identified as foundational issue
Wall exercises progressing well — 25 rep bilateral sequences

concerns.title: Breathing dysfunction
concerns.detail: Persistent thoracic extension compensation during breathing — diaphragm pulls down but unsecured ribs trigger elevation through low back extension. Foundational issue affecting all exercises.

concerns.title: Scapulothoracic interface dysfunction
concerns.detail: Left scapula elevated, 'bunch of fluff' tissue posterior preventing proper seating. Position-dependent clicking eliminated by forward lean.

concerns.title: Pectoral engagement severely limited (~2-3/10)
concerns.detail: Key recovery indicator; muscles guarding/spasming to protect unstable ribs. Compensatory patterns from 17 months post-surgery.

concerns.title: Left-chain compensation cascade
concerns.detail: Labral tear → rib instability → manual self-reorg → thumb MCP overload. Elite compensations from yoga teacher training deeply baked in.

sinceLastSession: Experimented with outdoor deck sleeping — improved positioning control
Continued balloon breathing but persistent chest breathing pattern
Persistent left shoulder/clavicle tension (1 week)
Resistance bands still not purchased
Maintained exercise compliance with available exercises
findings: Fundamental breathing mechanics dysfunction — chest breathing with rib elevation and lip pursing instead of diaphragmatic expansion
'Belly rise versus rib rise' identified as primary correction target
'Garter compression' analogy — maintain abdominal engagement while breathing diaphragmatically

Firmer sleeping surface experimentation yielding positive results
Resistance bands still not acquired — 3rd session deferred
